political.s: Manitowoc leans right with FEC donations showing 43,000 of 48,000 contributions to Republicans. Estimated PAC/lobbying spending indicates conservative political alignment consistent with industrial manufacturing sector interests.

charity.s: Manitowoc demonstrates solid charitable engagement with $50,000 donations to Brooke's House supporting women's recovery and workforce development. Company supports matching gift programs and community partnerships focused on employment opportunities.
charity.focus: workforce development, women's recovery programs, community engagement

environment.s: Environmental record is mixed with EPA enforcement action involving 1,420 engines related to emissions certification violations, resulting in $14.9 million in liabilities. Company named to America's Most Responsible Companies 2025 for third consecutive year and has implemented energy management systems, though enforcement history is concerning.

labor.s: Limited NLRB enforcement history with closed cases documented. Company emphasizes workplace safety and human rights commitment but sparse public data on labor relations. Two NLRB cases on record (2001 and 2023) provide minimal detail.

dei.s: Manitowoc publicly commits to DEI principles with dedicated initiatives, comprehensive policies, and training programs. Company emphasizes fairness, respect, and human rights at all organizational levels.

animals.s: Not applicable to crane and industrial equipment manufacturer.

guns.s: Not applicable to crane and industrial equipment manufacturer.

privacy.s: Insufficient public data on company data privacy practices and policies. No major privacy breach disclosures identified in available sources.

execPay.s: CEO Aaron Ravenscroft earned $6.7M in total compensation (2023). Compensation split shows 15% salary, 85% performance-based remuneration. CEO pay scrutinized given declining shareholder returns (-51% over three years) and reduced EPS.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
